Want to Start Vibe Coding? The Fundamentals Beginners Should Get Down First

Lately a lot of friends have been asking me, "I want to use AI to build things too — where do I start?" And what they're really asking about is Vibe Coding. This post is about the fundamentals beginners should get down before diving into Vibe Coding — Git, command line, UI Frameworks, and so on. So you don't get halfway through and find you're stuck not knowing how to communicate with the AI.
GitHub Pages Lets You Set Up Your Own 404 Page Too

GitHub Pages actually supports a custom 404 page out of the box — no extra config needed. This post explains what a 404 page is for (UX and SEO), what GitHub Pages' default 404 looks like, and how to drop a 404.html (or 404.md with permalink) into your repo root to ship your own.
The Simplest Way to Deploy Your Own gh-pages Site

gh-pages is a free GitHub service that lets you deploy a static site for showcase purposes. For frontend devs that's super handy. But there are a bunch of ways to deploy, so this post walks through the simplest one.
Git Filename Case Sensitivity Issues and How to Fix Them
Git treats readme.md and README.md as the same file on macOS or Windows, so renaming silently does nothing. How to reproduce the trap and two clean ways to fix it.
Migrating From Heroku to Render: A Deployment Guide

Heroku was my go-to backend platform for years, but in 2022/11/28 they killed the free tier. This post is the walkthrough I went through migrating my sites to Render.com — why I picked Render, how to deploy a Node.js project end to end (with environment variables), and the Render-specific quirks worth knowing about (sleep mode, shared monthly hours, slow free-tier deploys, the HTTP endpoint requirement, and bots that send twice).

Ever thought your Git commits look kind of ugly? Or you can't figure out what to write half the time? This post introduces git-cz, a tool that frees you from writing commit messages by hand and makes them prettier. Lazy developer's paradise — not even an exaggeration.
Fix: All Posts Show Updated Date After Hexo Upgrade or CI Deploy
After upgrading Hexo, every article started showing an "updated" date, even articles I hadn't touched. This post is on how to fix that — marking unmodified posts as updated is confusing for readers.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - Vite: Why It's So Fast & Deploying to GitHub Pages - (23)
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

In the last chapter we got our Vite + React + Tailwind CSS environment ready, so now it's time to move everything over from CRA to Vite. After that, we'll take a quick look at why Vite is so fast under the hood, and walk through two different ways to deploy the project to GitHub Pages.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - Moving the TodoList into CRA and Deploying to GitHub Pages - (21)

In this chapter we're taking the TodoList we built back on day eleven and moving it over from CodePen into a real Create React App (CRA) project. Along the way we'll tidy up the CRA boilerplate, add Tailwind CSS so the TodoList actually looks decent, and then walk through deploying the whole thing to GitHub Pages step by step. By the end you'll have a real, shareable URL for your TodoList.
Organize Your GitHub Stars With Tags

If you're a developer, you've probably starred plenty of projects on GitHub. But once you've starred a few hundred of them, finding the one you actually need turns into a nightmare. This post walks through how to organize all those Stars you've collected.
